 {"id":"phage_explorer-9jc","content_hash":"ab4a5f145952ff888140dec028b0ca9340c40af5000f61edb8c16ea32ef6473e","title":"Advanced: Cocktail Resistance Evolution Simulator","description":"Simulate resistance emergence under single vs cocktail regimens using Gillespie/tau-leaping. Params from genome: receptor diversity, Sie genes, CRISPR spacer matches. Live ASCII trajectories, risk meter, side-by-side mono vs cocktail. Turns genomic evidence into dosing/risk guidance. Novelty: High, Pedagogical: High (stochastic processes, evolutionary dynamics).","notes":"## COCKTAIL RESISTANCE EVOLUTION SIMULATOR

**Full Implementation from Roadmap**

### Concept
Simulate resistance emergence under single vs cocktail regimens using genome-derived parameters (receptor diversity, anti-defense, spacer proximity).

### How to Build

**Stochastic Engine:**
- Gillespie/tau-leaping in Rust+WASM (rand + rayon)
- Events: bacterial growth, phage adsorption, resistance mutation, receptor switching

**Genome-Derived Parameters:**
- Receptor diversity from tail fiber annotation
- Sie genes (superinfection exclusion)
- CRISPR spacer matches (immunity potential)
- Anti-defense gene count

**User Inputs:**
- MOI (multiplicity of infection)
- Dosing cadence (hours between doses)
- Population size (CFU/mL)
- Cocktail composition (1-5 phages)

**Outputs:**
- Probability of resistance over time
- Time-to-resistance distribution
- Compare mono vs cocktail curves
- Optimal cocktail selection

### TUI Features
- Live ASCII trajectories (multiple runs overlaid)
- Risk meter updates in real-time
- Side-by-side mono vs cocktail comparison
- Sliders: MOI, dose interval, population size
- Toggle 'optimize cocktail' to auto-pick best trio
- Resistance probability timeline
- [R]un new simulation, [C]ompare, [O]ptimize

### Why It's Good
- Turns genomic evidence into therapy guidance
- Quantifies resistance risk over time
- Compares mono vs cocktail robustness
- Identifies synergistic combinations

### Implementation Stack
- Rust+WASM for Gillespie simulator (rand, rayon)
- TS/Ink UI for sliders and live trajectories
- SQLite cache for genome-derived parameters
- ASCII braille for trajectory plots

 {"id":"phage_explorer-9u0s","content_hash":"ba6da6bc2b03f19afb6ceb25bfb2e9d08b08f9ce3f4dd2b876874cf99fb55fed","title":"Pillar 4: Visual Explainers (The Wow Factor)","description":"## Animated, Interactive Biological Visualizations

### Philosophy
These aren't just illustrations—they're interactive tools that make abstract biology tangible. The goal is to create 'aha moments' where complex concepts suddenly click.

### Visual 1: DNA Helix Explorer
**What it shows:**
- 3D double helix you can rotate/zoom
- Click nucleotides to see base pairing
- Follow the backbone (sugar-phosphate)
- See major/minor grooves
- Animate unwinding for replication

**Technical approach:**
- Three.js for 3D rendering
- Simplified model (10-20 base pairs)
- Annotations on hover
- 'DNA basics' link to module

### Visual 2: Translation Visualizer
**What it shows:**
- mRNA strand moving through ribosome
- tRNAs delivering amino acids
- Codon-anticodon matching
- Growing polypeptide chain
- Stop codon and release

**Technical approach:**
- Canvas 2D animation
- Step-by-step or continuous mode
- Current codon highlighted
- Speed control

### Visual 3: Codon Wheel/Table
**What it shows:**
- All 64 codons organized by first/second/third position
- Color by amino acid property (hydrophobic, polar, charged)
- Click codon → show amino acid info
- Search by amino acid → highlight codons

**Technical approach:**
- SVG or Canvas
- Two views: wheel and table
- Filter by property

### Visual 4: Phage Infection Animation
**What it shows:**
- Phage attaching to bacterial surface
- DNA injection (tail contraction)
- Replication inside host
- Assembly of new phages
- Lysis and release

**Technical approach:**
- Canvas 2D or CSS animation
- Timeline control (scrub through stages)
- Stage annotations
- Option for lytic vs lysogenic path

### Visual 5: Reading Frame Demo
**What it shows:**
- Same DNA sequence in all 3 forward frames
- Visual shift showing different amino acids
- Stop codon positions highlighted
- Why frame matters for ORF finding

**Technical approach:**
- Interactive sequence display
- Frame selector (1, 2, 3)
- Real-time translation update

### Files to Create
- packages/web/src/education/visualizers/DNAHelixViz.tsx
- packages/web/src/education/visualizers/TranslationViz.tsx
- packages/web/src/education/visualizers/CodonWheelViz.tsx
- packages/web/src/education/visualizers/InfectionViz.tsx
- packages/web/src/education/visualizers/ReadingFrameViz.tsx

### Integration
- Embedded in foundational modules
- Standalone access from 'Explore' menu
- Context panels can show relevant visualizer

### Why Prioritize Visuals
Biology textbooks are full of static diagrams. Interactive visualizations are what make concepts truly click. These create the 'beautiful' reaction that drives engagement and learning.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:33:17.735589-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-09T11:33:17.735589-05:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-9u0s","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-ty1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:33:17.751478-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"phage_explorer-9wby","content_hash":"6fe2f34ca3950f6782a470cdadb0bc2865b05298f46f576425b44b66efc577af","title":"Dual-row DNA+AA inline translation view","description":"# Task: Dual-row DNA+AA inline translation view

## Problem
Currently users must toggle between DNA and AA view modes. For codon-level analysis, seeing both simultaneously is essential—DNA sequence with its translation aligned below.

## Solution
Implement a dual-row rendering mode in CanvasSequenceGridRenderer that shows:

```
Position: 1    4    7    10   13   16   19...
DNA:      ATG  GAC  TTA  GCC  AAA  TGT  CAG...
AA:        M    D    L    A    K    C    Q ...
```

## Technical Design

### Layout Structure
- Row 1 (top): DNA triplets with codon grouping visual cues
- Row 2 (bottom): Single AA centered under each codon
- Visual separator line between rows
- Codon boundary markers (subtle vertical lines every 3bp)

### State Management
```typescript
// Add to store
viewMode: dna | aa | dual  // Add dual option

// Add to SequenceGridOptions
dualRowMode?: boolean;
codonGrouping?: boolean;  // Show visual codon boundaries
```

### Rendering Logic
```typescript
// In CanvasSequenceGridRenderer
private renderDualRow(ctx, range, layout) {
  const codonStart = Math.floor(range.startIndex / 3) * 3;
  
  for (let i = codonStart; i < range.endIndex; i += 3) {
    // Render DNA row (3 nucleotides)
    this.renderCodon(ctx, i, rowY: 0);
    
    // Render AA row (1 amino acid centered)
    const aa = translateCodon(sequence.slice(i, i+3));
    this.renderAA(ctx, aa, i, rowY: 1);
  }
}
```

## Files to Modify

- `/packages/web/src/rendering/CanvasSequenceGridRenderer.ts` - Add dual-row render path
- `/packages/state/src/store.ts` - Add dual view mode
- `/packages/web/src/components/SequenceView.tsx` - Add UI toggle for dual mode
- `/packages/web/src/hooks/useSequenceGrid.ts` - Support dual mode

## Cell Sizing for Dual Mode

| Zoom Level | DNA Cell | AA Cell | Total Row Height |
|------------|----------|---------|------------------|
| Codon (1.0x) | 8×10px | 24×12px | 24px |
| Base (2.0x) | 16×16px | 48×18px | 36px |
| Ultra (4.0x) | 32×24px | 96×28px | 56px |

## Visual Design

- DNA row: Standard nucleotide colors
- AA row: Property-based coloring (hydrophobic=yellow, polar=green, charged=blue/red)
- Codon boundaries: Subtle 1px line every 3bp
- Reading frame indicator: Highlight current frame offset

## Dependencies

- Core translateCodon function (exists in @phage-explorer/core)
- GlyphAtlas (for AA rendering)
- Store update for new view mode

 {"id":"phage_explorer-ujh","content_hash":"205b15c91b21d5b5a95cff6ff868f775d327ffc4b634d2dcc4160988cd775588","title":"Advanced: Protein Domain Chord Plot","description":"## Protein Domain Chord Plot (Advanced Feature)

### Concept

Phage proteomes are **modular mosaics** — built from shuffled and recombined functional domains. The same DNA-binding domain might appear in the repressor of one phage and the anti-repressor of another. The same tail fiber receptor-binding domain might be swapped between distant phages to change host range.

A **chord diagram** visualizes these domain-sharing relationships as arcs connecting phages. Each arc represents a shared domain, with thickness proportional to copy number or sequence identity. The result reveals:
- **Core domains**: Present in all phages (structural essentials)
- **Accessory domains**: Patchy distribution (niche adaptation)
- **Domain promiscuity**: Domains appearing in unexpected combinations
- **Convergent evolution**: Same domain, independent acquisitions

### Mathematical Foundations

**Domain Detection via HMM profiles:**
```
HMMER3 hmmscan workflow:
  1. Query each protein against Pfam-A HMM database
  2. For each domain hit:
     - E-value < 1e-5 (significant)
     - Coverage > 60% of HMM model
     - No envelope overlap with higher-scoring hits
Output: domain annotations with Pfam accession, name, positions, bit score
```

**Domain occurrence matrix:**
```
Let D = {d₁, d₂, ..., dₘ} = all detected domains
Let P = {p₁, p₂, ..., pₙ} = all phages

Occurrence matrix O[m × n]:
  O[i,j] = count of domain dᵢ in phage pⱼ

Presence/absence matrix B[m × n]:
  B[i,j] = 1 if O[i,j] > 0, else 0
```

**Domain co-occurrence (for clustering):**
```
Jaccard similarity between domains:
  J(dᵢ, dⱼ) = |phages with both| / |phages with either|

Domains with high Jaccard often function together.
Cluster with MCL (Markov Cluster Algorithm) to find domain modules.
```

**Chord diagram layout:**
```
Phages arranged on circle (0 to 2π): θᵢ = 2π × i / n
For each shared domain connecting phages pᵢ and pⱼ:
  Draw Bezier curve from θᵢ to θⱼ
  Control points at circle center (creates arc shape)
Arc thickness ∝ number of shared copies or sequence identity
```

### Implementation Approach

**Domain hit storage:**
```typescript
interface DomainHit {
  pfamId: string;
  pfamName: string;
  clanId?: string;
  proteinId: string;
  phageId: number;
  start: number;
  end: number;
  score: number;
  eValue: number;
  description: string;
}
```

**Domain sharing computation:**
```typescript
interface DomainSharing {
  domain: string;
  phageA: number;
  phageB: number;
  copiesA: number;
  copiesB: number;
  averageIdentity: number;
}

// Find all pairwise sharings across phages
// Group by domain family for filtering
```

**MCL clustering for domain modules:**
```typescript
interface DomainCluster {
  id: number;
  domains: string[];
  name: string;  // Inferred function
  phageCount: number;
}

// Build adjacency from co-occurrence Jaccard
// MCL iterations: expand (matrix multiply) + inflate (element-wise power)
// Extract clusters from converged matrix
```

**ASCII Braille chord renderer:**
```typescript
// Braille characters give 2x4 dots per cell = high resolution curves
// placeBraillePoint() sets individual dots in character grid
// Bezier curves drawn point-by-point through center
// Result: smooth arcs connecting phages on outer ring
```

### TUI Visualization

```
Protein Domain Chord Plot: Lambda + T4 + T7 + P22 + Phi29
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

              Lambda
                ╱╲
              ╱    ╲
           ⡠⠊      ⠑⡄
         ⡰⠁          ⠑⡄
       ⡠⠃              ⠘⡄
      ⡜                  ⠘⡄           Selected: HTH_3 (Helix-turn-helix)
    ⡰⠁                    ⠑⡄           Found in: Lambda (cI), P22 (c2)
   ⡜                        ⠘⡄          Function: DNA-binding repressor
  ⡜                          ⠘⡄
 ⡔    T4                 P22   ⠢⡀
⡎      ⠑⢄                ⡠⠊      ⠱⡀
⡇        ⠑⢄            ⡠⠊        ⠱⡀
⡇          ⠑⢄        ⡠⠊          ⢱
⡇            ⠑⢄    ⡠⠊            ⢱
⡇              ⠑⢄⡠⠊              ⢱
⠘⡄              ⡜               ⡰⠁
 ⠘⡄            ⡜               ⡰⠁
  ⠘⡄          ⡜               ⡰⠁
   ⠑⡄        ⡜              ⡰⠁
    ⠑⡄      ⡜   Phi29     ⡠⠊
     ⠑⡄    ⡜            ⡠⠊
       ⠑⡄⡰⠁          ⡠⠊
         ⠱⡀        ⡠⠊
           T7───────⠁

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Domain Summary:
┌─────────────┬──────────────────────────────┬────────┬─────────────────┐
│ Domain      │ Phages                       │ Copies │ Function        │
├─────────────┼──────────────────────────────┼────────┼─────────────────┤
│ Phage_cap_E │ Lambda, T4, T7, P22, Phi29   │   8    │ Capsid protein  │
│ HTH_3       │ Lambda, P22                  │   2    │ DNA binding     │
│ Phage_tail  │ Lambda, T4, P22              │  12    │ Tail structure  │
│ DNA_pol_B   │ T4, T7, Phi29                │   3    │ DNA replication │
│ Peptidase_M │ Lambda, T4, T7, P22          │   4    │ Lysis           │
└─────────────┴──────────────────────────────┴────────┴─────────────────┘

Legend: ━ Core (all phages)  ─ Common (3-4)  ╌ Rare (2)

[Tab] Cycle domains  [Enter] Show alignment  [F] Filter by clan  [ESC] Exit
```

### Why This Is Good

1. **Domain-level evolution**: Genes evolve, but domains are truly modular units. Domain sharing reveals deeper relationships.
2. **Function transfer mechanism**: Which domains spread through phage populations.
3. **Protein engineering hints**: Domain X appears with domain Y = may function together.
4. **Beautiful data reduction**: Thousands of genes → dozens of domains → one elegant visualization.
5. **Novel TUI visualization**: Chord diagrams in terminals are rare; Braille curves push ASCII art boundaries.

### Innovation Assessment

**Novelty: HIGH** - Chord diagrams exist (Circos), but no TUI implementation. ASCII/Braille curve rendering is novel. Interactive domain filtering is new.

**Pedagogical Value: 9/10** - Teaches protein domains, HMM profiles, modular evolution, network visualization.

**Wow Factor: 9/10** - Smooth Braille curves connecting distant phages through shared domains is visually stunning.

### Data Requirements
- HMMER + Pfam-A database for domain detection (offline preprocessing)
- Pre-computed domain_hits table in SQLite
- ~50MB additional data for Pfam profiles","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:20:17.342284-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-06T23:43:59.044724-05:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-ujh","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-54g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:20:17.344679-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-upat","content_hash":"ba2b231613e0c2779920dd17d565950e3cb83d1ae58ae41a641549a5da3b3bcd","title":"Phase 5: Simulations","description":"## Overview

Integrate the 6 simulation engines that model phage biology in real-time, allowing users to explore dynamic biological processes.

## Why Simulations Matter

Static sequence analysis shows **what** a phage is. Simulations show **how** it behaves:

1. **Lysogeny Decision** - Lambda switch between lysis and lysogeny
2. **Ribosome Traffic** - Translation dynamics and codon effects
3. **Plaque Formation** - Spatial spread through bacterial lawn
4. **Evolution** - Mutation and selection over generations
5. **Infection Kinetics** - Population dynamics during infection
6. **Packaging Motor** - DNA packaging into capsid

These simulations make abstract genomic features tangible and educational.

## Current State

### Visualizers (Exist in web)
- ✅ LysogenyVisualizer - CI/Cro concentration dynamics
- ✅ RibosomeVisualizer - Ribosome traffic flow
- ✅ PlaqueVisualizer - 2D cellular automata
- ✅ EvolutionVisualizer - Phylogenetic replay
- ✅ TimeControls - Play/pause/speed controls

### Missing
- ❌ InfectionKineticsVisualizer
- ❌ PackagingMotorVisualizer
- ❌ SimulationView - Unified container

### Infrastructure (Exists)
- ✅ useSimulation hook
- ✅ SimulationHub overlay
- ⚠️ Worker integration (partial)

## Technical Components

### 1. SimulationView Container

**What**: Unified view for displaying active simulation

**Features**:
- Simulation selector (if not from hub)
- Parameter controls (rate constants, initial conditions)
- Real-time state display
- Time controls (play/pause/speed/step)
- Reset functionality

### 2. Worker Integration

**What**: Connect simulation engines to Web Workers

**Why workers**: Simulations can be CPU-intensive; blocking the main thread kills UI responsiveness

**Pattern**:
```typescript
// In worker
const sim = new LysogenySimulation(params);
while (running) {
  sim.step();
  postMessage({ state: sim.getState() });
  await sleep(16); // 60fps target
}

// In component
const state = useSimulation('lysogeny', params);
```

### 3. Missing Visualizers

**InfectionKineticsVisualizer**:
- Population curves (uninfected, infected, lysed, phage)
- Phase plane portrait
- Rate parameter sliders

**PackagingMotorVisualizer**:
- DNA progress into capsid
- Motor force dynamics
- Stall detection

## Simulation Parameters

Each simulation needs parameter UI:

| Simulation | Key Parameters |
|------------|----------------|
| Lysogeny | CI/Cro synthesis rates, degradation rates, initial MOI |
| Ribosome | Elongation rate, initiation rate, codon bias effects |
| Plaque | Infection rate, burst size, diffusion constant |
| Evolution | Mutation rate, selection coefficient, population size |
| Infection | Adsorption rate, latent period, burst size |
| Packaging | Motor force, friction, DNA length |

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: Phase 1 (need sequence data for some simulations)
- **Blocks**: None

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All 6 simulations accessible from SimulationHub
- [ ] Simulations run in worker (smooth UI)
- [ ] Can adjust parameters and see effects
- [ ] TimeControls work (play/pause/speed)
- [ ] Can reset simulation to initial state
- [ ] State persists when switching views

## Files to Create/Modify

```
packages/web/src/
├── components/
│   └── SimulationView.tsx                # NEW
├── components/simulations/
│   ├── InfectionKineticsVisualizer.tsx   # NEW
│   └── PackagingMotorVisualizer.tsx      # NEW
├── workers/
│   └── simulationWorker.ts               # ENHANCE
```

## Estimated Complexity: Medium

Most visualizers exist.  {"id":"phage_explorer-vru3","content_hash":"ce23fee61cc8443c5e7c20813a127d4d1eab7eb30158beec1f307738695f298d","title":"Feature: 3D Rendering Overhaul","description":"","notes":"# Feature: 3D Rendering Overhaul

## Executive Summary

A comprehensive overhaul of the 3D molecular structure viewer to address critical usability and visual quality issues.

## Current Problems

1. **Zoom-to-Extents Broken**: Molecules don't auto-fit to viewport (THIRD TIME REQUESTED)
2. **Poor Atom Coloring**: Ball-and-stick mode uses colors that are hard to distinguish
3. **Redundant Render Mode**: Cartoon mode is nearly identical to Ribbon
4. **Missing Phages**: Some catalog phages may not appear with 3D models

## Sub-Tasks

### P0 - Critical
- `phage_explorer-de4o`: Fix zoom-to-extents auto-fit

### P1 - High Priority
- `phage_explorer-qm08`: Implement CPK element coloring
- `phage_explorer-z49z`: Verify all phages have 3D models

### P2 - Normal Priority
- `phage_explorer-xkxf`: Remove redundant Cartoon mode
- `phage_explorer-k3l5`: Add functional group visualization

## Technical Context

**File Locations:**
- Model viewer: `/packages/web/src/components/Model3DView.tsx`
- Structure loader: `/packages/web/src/visualization/structure-loader.ts`
- Phage catalog: `/packages/data-pipeline/src/phage-catalog.ts`

**Key Technologies:**
- Three.js for WebGL rendering
- React Query for structure fetching
- RCSB PDB for structure data

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All molecules auto-fit to viewport on load
- [ ] Atoms clearly distinguishable by element type
- [ ] Only 3 render modes (Ball-Stick, Ribbon, Surface)
- [ ] All 12 catalog phages have working 3D models
- [ ] Functional groups highlighted in complex structures

## Definition of Done

All sub-tasks completed and tested on multiple phages across different device sizes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:25:34.423964-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-09T11:34:11.514737-05:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-vru3","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-jjq5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:26:24.298042-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-vs9k","content_hash":"c88d05fa88fc226763f3d80921c80d361e93499d450fdd81fc0c516f0a9fe08d","title":"Pillar 5: Context-Aware Help System","description":"## Smart Explanations That Appear When Relevant

### Philosophy
Instead of requiring users to seek out help, context-aware explanations appear automatically based on what the user is viewing. This creates a 'just-in-time learning' experience.

### How It Works
1. **InfoButton Component** - Small [?] icons placed next to technical displays
2. **ContextPanel Component** - Slides in with relevant explanation
3. **useContextHelp Hook** - Determines what explanation to show based on current view

### Context Triggers

**When viewing DNA sequence:**
- What are these letters? (A, T, G, C)
- Why are some colored differently?
- What does the position number mean?

**When viewing amino acids:**
- How did DNA become amino acids?
- What do the colors represent?
- What is a reading frame?

**When viewing genes:**
- What is a gene/ORF?
- What do start/end positions mean?
- What is a locus tag?

**When using analysis overlays:**
- GC Skew: Why does GC content vary? What does skew indicate?
- Complexity: What is sequence entropy? Why does it matter?
- Repeats: What are direct/inverted repeats? Why important?
- HGT: What is horizontal gene transfer?
- CRISPR: What is CRISPR? How do spacers work?
- Synteny: What is gene order conservation?
- Dot Plot: How do I read this matrix?

### Implementation

**ContextPanel Component:**
- Slides in from right side
- Title, explanation text, diagrams
- Links to: glossary terms, full module, visualizer
- Dismiss button, auto-dismiss on navigation

**InfoButton Component:**
- Consistent [?] icon style
- Hover preview (short), click for full panel
- Only visible in Beginner Mode

**ContextProvider:**
- Wraps app, tracks current context
- Provides `showContext(topicId)` function
- Manages panel open/close state

### Content Structure
Each context help entry has:
- `id`: unique identifier
- `title`: what is this?
- `shortExplanation`: 1-2 sentences
- `fullExplanation`: detailed (2-3 paragraphs)
- `glossaryTerms`: linked terms
- `relatedModule`: link to foundational module
- `visualizer`: optional interactive element

### Files to Create
- packages/web/src/education/context/ContextPanel.tsx
- packages/web/src/education/context/ContextProvider.tsx
- packages/web/src/education/components/InfoButton.tsx
- packages/web/src/education/context/topics/*.ts (content files)
- packages/web/src/education/hooks/useContextHelp.ts

### Integration Points
- SequenceView: add info buttons for DNA/AA display
- GeneMapCanvas: add info for gene annotations
- Each overlay: add context explanation header
- ComparisonOverlay: explain metrics

### Why This Pillar Matters
This is the 'magic' of Beginner Mode—help appears exactly when needed without the user having to search. It transforms the app from 'tool for experts' to 'learning environment for everyone'.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:41:56.750332-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-09T11:41:56.750332-05:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-vs9k","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-ty1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:41:56.751579-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-vs9k","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-0weo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:41:56.753088-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
